en.extract:
The square-tailed saw-wing (Psalidoprocne nitens), also known as the square-tailed rough-winged swallow is a species of bird in the family Hirundinidae.
It is native to the African tropical rainforest.


Description
It is dark green, almost black. 


Subspecies
There are currently two recognised subspecies.

P. n. nitens, the nominate subspecies, which occurs in Guinea, Sierra Leone, Ivory Coast, Ghana, Togo, Nigeria, Gabon, Congo and Angola.
P. n. centralis, which occurs in northeast Democratic Republic of the Congo.


References

The '''square-tailed saw-wing''' ('''''Psalidoprocne nitens'''''), also known as the '''square-tailed rough-winged swallow''' is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Hirundinidae]].
It is native to the [[Tropical rainforest|African tropical rainforest]].

== Description ==
It is dark green, almost black.<ref>{{Cite journal |last=Fishpool |first=Lincoln D. C. |date=2023-09-07 |title=Square-tailed Saw-wing Psalidoprocne nitens in the Democratic Republic of the Congo: identity and taxonomic status |journal=Bulletin of the British Ornithologists' Club |volume=143 |issue=3 |doi=10.25226/bboc.v143i3.2023.a5 |issn=0007-1595|doi-access=free }}</ref> 

==Subspecies==
There are currently two recognised subspecies.<ref>{{cite web |title=Square-tailed Sawwing |url=https://birdsoftheworld.org/bow/species/sqtsaw1/cur/introduction |website=Birds of the World |publisher=The Cornell Lab of Ornithology |access-date=20 January 2021}}</ref>
*''P. n. nitens'', the [[nominate subspecies]], which occurs in Guinea, Sierra Leone, Ivory Coast, Ghana, Togo, Nigeria, Gabon, Congo and Angola.  
*''P. n. centralis'', which occurs in northeast Democratic Republic of the Congo.
